item.description:
I am a physics PhD student at the University of Pittsburgh. My research interests lie in both theoretical and observational cosmology. In particular, I am interested in contraining cosmological models through their imprints on the cosmic microwave background (CMB). One project that I am currently working on is to understand the impacts of primordial magnetic field on the CMB anisotropies. Specifically, how does primordial magnetic field affect the B-mode signal.

I am also interested in CMB data analysis. As a member of two collaborations: ACT (Atacama Cosmology Telescope) and SO (Simons Observatory), I am working on detector calibrations algorithms and data selection algorithms.
